PROJECT LITRE OF LIGHT- AN INITIATIVE AIMING TOWARDS ILLUMINATING INDIA
RENEWABLY
 Project Litre of Light is a grassroots movement led by me in my school, started as an initiative to
provide affordable, sustainable lighting solutions to underprivileged communities lacking access to
electricity. The initiative was inspired by a simple yet innovative idea: transforming plastic bottles into
solar-powered light bulbs.
 The project utilizes readily available materials, primarily plastic bottles, and solar panels to create a
solar-powered light source, a powerful compact lantern.
 The materials used are low-cost and readily available, making this a cost-effective solution for
communities with limited resources. The cost of one bulb rounds off to a total of  150.
 We aim to provide solar-powered light bulbs to underdeveloped areas near Dehradun which lack easy
access to electricity. We were successful and graced enough to be able to light up the world of many
families in Thakurpur district of Dehradun and we aim to continue the good work.
